title: Front-end Architect
tagline: Building scalable, high-performance enterprise UIs with Angular, React, and modern design systems.
metaDescription: Portfolio of Annamalai Kathiresan, a Senior Front-end Architect specializing in Angular, React, micro-frontends, and enterprise UI architecture.

pillars.title: Architecture & Performance
pillars.description: Expert in scalable UI architecture — monolithic and micro-frontends. Advanced performance strategies including code splitting, lazy loading, and smart caching.

pillars.title: Design Systems & UX
pillars.description: Pixel-perfect, inclusive interfaces with design system integration, responsive layouts, and Core Web Vitals optimization.

pillars.title: Technical Leadership
pillars.description: Driving Agile delivery, mentoring teams, conducting code reviews, and bridging stakeholders with engineering.

items.title: Tata Communications
items.description: Lead Front-end Architect for enterprise Angular applications. Designed scalable micro-frontends architecture, optimized bundle performance, and managed CI/CD via GitHub Actions.

items.title: Comcast Corporation
items.description: Engineered Bulk Invite and Bulk Modem Restart features, improving onboarding efficiency by 75%. Advanced RxJS patterns and NgRx for complex state management.

items.title: ETS — TOEFL Learn
items.description: Cross-platform mobile app with React Native. AWS Amplify & Cognito authentication. Achieved 90% code reuse across iOS and Android.

items.title: Verizon Communications
items.description: Responsive dashboards with Angular and PrimeNG. Adobe Analytics integration. Reduced load times by 30% through rendering optimization.
Lead Front-end Architect for enterprise products. Delivering licensed Angular products, driving architecture decisions, accessibility compliance, and CI/CD implementation.
Front-end development across Verizon, ETS, and Comcast. Specialized in Angular and React ecosystems, delivering high-impact features and performance optimizations.

contact.message: I'm always open to discussing architecture challenges, design system work, or opportunities to lead front-end engineering teams.
